subject: dualsense: add lightbar color control.
author: Sorgelig

upstream.vanilla_quote: L200: `struct led_classdev_mc lightbar;` — one multicolor LED classdev, not three separate ones. L862-899 `ps_lightbar_register()`: allocates a 3-entry `mc_subled` array (`LED_COLOR_ID_RED/GREEN/BLUE`), sets `lightbar_mc_dev->subled_info`/`num_colors = 3`, names the single node `led_cdev->name = devm_kasprintf(&hdev->dev, GFP_KERNEL, "%s:rgb:indicator", ps_dev->input_dev_name)`, and registers it with `devm_led_classdev_multicolor_register(&hdev->dev, lightbar_mc_dev)`. L1197-1211 `dualsense_lightbar_set_brightness()`: calls `led_mc_calc_color_components(mc_cdev, brightness)` then reads `mc_cdev->subled_info[0/1/2].brightness` for red/green/blue and forwards to `dualsense_set_lightbar()`. L1841-1848 in `dualsense_create()`: `ret = ps_lightbar_register(ps_dev, &ds->lightbar, dualsense_lightbar_set_brightness);` followed by `dualsense_set_lightbar(ds, 0, 0, 128); /* blue */` — vanilla's out-of-the-box default color is blue, identical to the fork's default.

impact.effect_if_absent: No functional loss: dropping this fork commit and taking vanilla 6.18.38 as-is gives DualSense lightbar RGB control back, just through a different sysfs shape. Fork: three independent led_classdev nodes /sys/class/leds/<hid-devname>:red/, :green/, :blue/ (each plain 0-255 brightness, no color semantics). Vanilla: one led_classdev_mc node /sys/class/leds/<input-devname>:rgb:indicator/ using the standard multicolor LED ABI (multi_intensity for the R:G:B ratio, brightness for overall level). Userspace that wants to set a lightbar color must target a different set of sysfs paths, but the capability itself (arbitrary RGB lightbar color from userspace) is fully preserved and the default color on boot (blue, 0,0,128) is identical in both.

userspace_coupling.interface: LED sysfs. Fork exposes three separate classdevs (…:red, …:green, …:blue); vanilla exposes one multicolor classdev (…:rgb:indicator). Confirmed by exhaustive grep of /mnt/source/Main_MiSTer for lightbar/RGB-LED/multicolor/dualsense sysfs usage: the ONLY /sys/class/leds/ reference in the entire tree is `input.cpp:4052: #define LED_MONITOR "/sys/class/leds/hps_led0/brightness_hw_changed"`, which is the unrelated HPS status LED (board LED, not a controller lightbar). Main_MiSTer never reads or writes any DualSense/dualshock/lightbar LED path in either the fork's or vanilla's naming scheme.

forward_port.conflicts: Do not carry this commit forward. Vanilla 6.18.38 already ships a complete, standard-API lightbar implementation (led_classdev_mc, drivers/hid/hid-playstation.c:200/862-899/1197-1211/1841-1848) that supersedes it. Our own board/mister/de10nano/linux.config already sets CONFIG_LEDS_CLASS_MULTICOLOR=y (line 387), so the kconfig prerequisite for vanilla's approach is already satisfied on our side — no config gap to close. (Note: stock's config, docs/stock-inventory/stock-linux.config:2969, has `# CONFIG_LEDS_CLASS_MULTICOLOR is not set`, i.e. stock's older kernel predates/omits this — irrelevant to us since we already carry it.)
forward_port.effort: none — take vanilla's file as-is; nothing to port, no interface shim needed since no consumer targets the fork's :red/:green/:blue paths

superseded_by: fc97b4d6a1a6 (HID: playstation: expose DualSense lightbar through a multi-color LED, 2021-09-08, upstream in v5.16)

notes: PROVENANCE DOC IS WRONG for this row. Verbatim, patch-provenance.md:337: '| `f84543926`, `0d60c3482`, `60e08955f`, `b76b4bc6a` | DualSense player LEDs / lightbar / mute / player-6 | **`8c0ab553b072`** *HID: playstation: expose DualSense player LEDs through LED class* (2021-09-08); **`8e5198a12d64`** *…add initial DualSense lightbar support* (2021-02-16) | 6.18 `hid-playstation.c:217` `update_player_leds`, `:155` lightbar flag. MiSTer's were pre-upstream backports. ⚠ verify controller LED behaviour on HW (P3.13) |'. Two errors: (1) it GROUPS four textually-and-functionally distinct fork commits (player-id LED, lightbar RGB, mute button/LED, player-6 LED config) into one row cited against only two upstream SHAs — this is exactly the grouping failure mode this recon project exists to eliminate; each of the four needs its own record (0d60c3482 is this one). (2) For 0d60c3482 specifically, neither cited SHA is the actual match. `8e5198a12d64` (2021-02-16, confirmed ancestor of v6.18.38 via `git merge-base --is-ancestor`) only sets a hardcoded default lightbar color at probe time — it provides NO userspace-adjustable control, so it cannot be what 0d60c3482 (July 2021, adds a full 3-LED userspace-writable interface) 'backports'. The commit that actually gives userspace lightbar control is `fc97b4d6a1a6` (2021-09-08, also confirmed ancestor of v6.18.38), which lands upstream TWO MONTHS AFTER the fork commit and uses a materially different mechanism (one led_classdev_mc vs. three led_classdev). So 0d60c3482 is not a backport of anything — it is Sorgelig's own independent pre-upstream implementation of the same feature, which vanilla then solved differently and later. Given (a) vanilla provides the same user-facing capability (arbitrary RGB lightbar color from userspace, same blue default), (b) via a different but not inferior interface (led_classdev_mc is the standard kernel ABI for multicolor LEDs, arguably cleaner than three independent classdevs), and (c) confirmed zero Main_MiSTer coupling to the fork's :red/:green/:blue paths — this is graded `dropped-upstream`/`superseded-better`, not `misclassified`. Reserving `misclassified` for cases where the doc's error conceals a genuine regression (e.g. the DualSense mute→BTN_Z commit 60e08955f, part of this same grouped row, which IS a real uncarried regression per prior analysis) keeps that flag meaningful; using it here for a same-capability interface swap would dilute it. sonnet-verified: dropped-upstream/superseded-better, doc disagreement confirmed and re-derived independently — vanilla_shas corrected to fc97b4d6a1a6 only (8e5198a12d64 removed, it is not the matching commit), device_ids corrected to 054C:0CE6/054C:0DF2 (prior record had non-existent IDs 054C:CE6E/054C:DF11), fork LED registration and vanilla multicolor registration both quoted with file:line, Main_MiSTer grep re-run and confirmed zero coupling.
